title: Ep. 277 вЂ“ Across the Decades: Ram Dass on Service and Social Action
summary: Speaking across the decades, from the 1960s to the 2010s, Ram Dass shares his insights into responding to suffering, the meaning of service, and the confluence of social action and spiritual work. The Ram Dass community gathers regularly to engage in meaningful discussions about the podcast. We invite you to join us and share your curiosities, insights, and wisdom. Sign up for the General Fellowship to receive event invitations directly in your inbox. This episode of Here and Now is a compilation of Ram Dass talking about service and social action across the decades. We begin in 1969, during a time of significant cultural change. A time where the people of the United States found themselves in the middle of the Civil Rights Movement, the anti-war protests, and the rise of WomenвЂ™s Liberation. Ram Dass explores the concept of social responsibility and talks about why protesting should come from a place not of anger, but of love.Next, we move to 1983. The media landscape has transformed in the wake of the Iran hostage crisis, political paradigms shift as Ronald Reagan makes his way to power, and communities all over the world begin to feel the impact of the growing AIDS epidemic. Ram Dass talks about learning to trust oneвЂ™s intuitive inner voice when it comes to responding to suffering, and how we can bring together social action and spiritual work.Two years later, it is 1985 and the world has rapidly evolved. The Soviet Union has become a global threat. The nightly news shows the Apartheid regime in South Africa violently cracking down on Civil Rights activists, while the Reagan administration stands by, focused instead on rolling back civil liberties at home in the United States. Ram Dass offers perspective on navigating these challenges with an open heart. He explores the difference between dharma and seva, and why service requires us to embrace paradox in our lives.It is 1993, technology is transforming the world and how we engage with it.В  Ram Dass explores how being too attached to the fruits of our actions can be detrimental to social action work, leading activists to burn out quickly.We end our journey across the decades in 2018, in the middle of the first Trump administration in America. Wars rage on, and civil liberty is at risk across the globe. How do we oppose this skillfully and with an open heart? Ram Dass talks about how karma yoga is the key to finding the right balance between working on yourself and taking action for the benefit of others.В Sponsors of this Episode: Ram Dass Here & Now is brought to you byВ BetterHelp. Give online therapy a try atВ betterhelp.com/ramdassВ and get on your way to being your best self. This show is also sponsored byВ Magic Mind, a matcha-based energy shot infused with nootropics and adaptogens designed to crush procrastination, brain fog, & fatigue. You have a limited offer you can use now, that gets you up toВ 48% offВ your first subscription orВ 20% offВ one-time purchases with the codeВ RAMDASSВ at www.magicmind.com/ramdass. ReunionВ is offeringВ $250В off any stay to the Love, Serve, Remember community. Simply use the code вЂњBeHere250вЂќ when booking. Disconnect from the world so you can reconnect with yourself at Reunion.В В Hotel |В www.reunionhotelandwellness.comВ Retreats |В www.reunionexperience.org вЂњSo it really requires, it seems to me, staying open from moment to moment when youвЂ™re doing social action. And if youвЂ™re too obsessed with the goal, you lose it. If youвЂ™re too obsessed with the goal, since in much action you donвЂ™t get what you want, youвЂ™ll burn out much sooner. And so, the injunction of the Bhagavad Gita, which says be not identified with being the actor, be not attached to the fruits of the action, and yet, the action happens.вЂќ вЂ“ Ram Dass See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

title: Ram Katha 09: HG Amogh Lila Prabhu
summary: Amogh Lila Prabhu, also known as Amogh Lila Das, is a well-known spiritual leader, motivational speaker, lifestyle coach, and engineer. He has served as the vice president of the ISKCON Temple in Dwarka, Delhi. His speeches, often infused with humor and insight, have inspired many to pursue a spiritually enriched life.Originally named Ashish Arora, he was born in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh, and earned a B.Tech in Computer Science from Delhi College of Engineering. After a successful career in the corporate world, he chose the path of monastic life as a Hare Krishna Brahmachari.He has also built a strong presence on social media, where his spiritual and motivational content continues to resonate with a wide audience. Let me know if you'd like to discuss his teachings or explore related ideas!

title: Gospel | Chapter 3: Aug 5, 1882 (Part 4) | Swami Sarvapriyananda
summary: Swami Sarvapriyananda reads and discusses the Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna. The Gospel of Sri Ramakrishna is an English translation of the Bengali spiritual text Sri Sri Ramakrishna Kathamrita by Swami Nikhilananda. The text records conversations of Ramakrishna with his disciples, devotees and visitors, recorded by Mahendranath Gupta, who wrote the book under the pseudonym of "M." This session was recorded on Jun 15, 2021.

title: Episode 22
summary: Sri Brihad-bhagavatamritaВ is an epic work originally composed in Sanskrit during the sixteenth century byВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Sanatana GoswamiвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ , one ofВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Chaitanya MahaprabhuвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ 's leading disciples. It is one of the first books written by any of theВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Six Goswamis of Vrindavan.вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ In three volumes, it gives an engaging account of two great spiritual quests. In one, the Vedic sage Narada searches throughout the universe for Krishna's greatest devotee. In the other, the cowherd boy Gopa Kumar searches forВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Krishna HimselfвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЂ”the Supreme Person, the ultimate goal of life.TheВ BhagavatamritaВ is rooted in ancient history. It is the appendix to the sage Jaimini Rishi's version of the historical sagaВ Mahabharata, and was spoken by the hero of theВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Srimad-BhagavatamвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ , King Pariksit, to his mother, just moments before the king's death.This edition ofВ Sri Brihad-bhagavatamritaВ is the work of Gopiparanadhana Dasa, who was part of the team ofВ вЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ Srila Prabhupada'sвЃ вЃ вЃ вЃ В scholarly disciples who completed the translation and commentary on Srila Prabhupada's life's workвЂ”the Srimad-BhagavatamвЂ”after Srila Prabhupada's passing in 1977. Gopiparanadhana Dasa wrote several volumes of translation and commentary on the Goswamis' writings before his passing in 2011.

title: Episode 503: Chapter 12, verses 7,8 and 9
summary: Bhagavad Gita Ch. 12 вЂњYoga ofВ DevotionвЂќ Verses 7, 8, & 9 The lecture discusses KrishnaвЂ™s assurance that he will rescue his devotees from the ocean of ever-changing samsara, fraught with death and distraction. He offersВ  Abhyasa Yoga as an alternative for those who cannot fix their minds on the divine.
